item.title: Private School Charging $76,000 Offers Discount in Revival Plan
item.description: A Colorado private school has tapped the debt markets for $27 million to fund campus upgrades while offering discounts on tuition as part of a turnaround plan.

item.title: Blue Owl BDC Won Big New Investment by University of California
item.description: A $200 billion investment fund for the University of California system added almost 30 million shares of Blue Owl Technology Finance Corp. in the first quarter, in a sign of institutions seizing upon volatility in the private credit market.

item.title: Fed’s Goolsbee Says All Interest-Rate Options Are On The Table
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago President Austan Goolsbee said all options on interest rates are on the table right now.

item.title: AI Chipmaker Cerebras Is Said to Plan Raising IPO Price Range
item.description: Cerebras Systems Inc. is set to increase the price range of its initial public offering as soon as Monday, according to people familiar with the matter, as demand for the artificial intelligence chipmaker’s shares continues to build.

item.title: QVC Shareholders Claim Firm Giving Away Their Value to Creditors
item.description: Shareholders of bankrupt television shopping network QVC have accused the company of wrongly trying to give away hundreds of millions dollars worth of assets to other stakeholders as part of a proposal to cut debt and end the company’s insolvency case.

item.title: Google’s Isomorphic Labs to Raise Over $2 Billion in New Funding
item.description: Isomorphic Labs, an AI-powered drug discovery company spun out of Alphabet Inc.’s Google DeepMind, is in advanced discussions to raise more than $2 billion in a new round of funding, according to people familiar with the plan.

item.title: Musk, Altman Management Styles Come Under Fire at OpenAI Trial
item.description: Elon Musk and Sam Altman both took a beating over their leadership styles in court testimony this week as a jury waded further into their OpenAI feud.

item.title: NYC School District Hit by Malware Attack as Well as Canvas Hack
item.description: As the New York City Public School system grapples with the Canvas service hack affecting schools nationwide, one of its Manhattan campuses is contending with a separate incident.

item.title: KNDS IPO Wobbles Over Valuation as Peers Fall
item.description: KNDS NV’s plans for a summer initial public offering could be in doubt because of concerns about the tank maker’s valuation, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Milken Crowd Hates the Phrase ‘Semi-Liquid’ After Retail Exodus
item.description: ‘Semi-liquid is kind of a diabolical name’

item.title: German Banks Look to Offload Troubled Loan on LA Office Tower
item.description: German lenders are looking to sell a non-performing loan on the CalEdison Building in downtown Los Angeles, which has struggled to attract tenants.

item.title: Anthropic Inks $1.8 Billion Computing Deal With Akamai
item.description: Anthropic PBC has signed a $1.8 billion computing deal with cloud services provider Akamai Technologies Inc. to meet surging demand for its artificial intelligence software, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Cuba Faces ‘Devastating’ Ripple Effects From US Hit to Mining
item.description: Canadian miner Sherritt International Corp.’s decision to shut down its nickel operations in Cuba under US duress will weigh heavily on an economy that’s already starved for hard currency and fuel.

item.title: Apollo, Blackstone Weigh $35 Billion Financing for Broadcom
item.description: Apollo Global Management Inc. and Blackstone Inc. are among private credit lenders involved in talks with chipmaker Broadcom Inc. over a roughly $35 billion financing, the latest indication that companies are mobilizing all sources of capital to fund the AI build-out.

item.title: ‘Grand Theft Auto VI’ Publisher Looks to be ‘Smarter’ at Making Games
item.description: Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick says that some games need to be cheaper to make or they won’t be made at all

item.title: Ghana’s Credit Rating Upgraded by Fitch on Improved Finances
item.description: Fitch Ratings upgraded Ghana’s sovereign credit rating on the government’s improved fiscal management and increased foreign reserves, even as the war in the Middle East threatens to derail the nation’s economic recovery.

item.title: Higher Colombia Rates Won’t Tame Inflation, Central Banker Says
item.description: Colombia’s central bank should avoid additional interest rate hikes because tight monetary policy is no longer the correct tool to fight inflation, according to board member Cesar Giraldo.

item.title: Venezuela’s Faulty Power Grid Risks Derailing Economic Comeback
item.description: Venezuela announced emergency measures to stabilize its power grid after electricity consumption hit a nine-year high this week, reviving memories of blackouts and rationing that once crippled the economy.

item.title: Bradesco BBI Sees Brazil IPO Rebound After Compass Debut
item.description: The successful initial public offering of Compass Gas e Energia SA, the first in Brazil in nearly five years, means the country’s equity capital markets have now reopened even as global volatility keeps a cap on the pace of new deals, according to Banco Bradesco BBI.

item.title: Yahoo Pays Up to Refinance $1.6 Billion Debt Tied to Apollo Deal
item.description: Yahoo Inc. raised $1.6 billion from a loan and bond deal to refinance debt tied to its acquisition by Apollo Global Management after offering investors one of the highest-yielding deals of the year.

item.title: ECB Is ‘Highly Vigilant’ to Rising Inflation Risks, Nagel Says
item.description: The European Central Bank is “highly vigilant” to rising inflation risks due to the Iran war and will act as needed to prevent higher energy costs spilling over into prices more broadly, Governing Council member Joachim Nagel said.

item.title: Some College Finals Delayed After Canvas Online Platform Hacked
item.description: A breach of a third-party online platform that is widely used by primary schools and colleges has disrupted classes and finals just as the academic year is coming to a close.

item.title: Saudi Delivery App Ninja Taps Banks for $1 Billion IPO
item.description: Saudi startup Ninja has picked a roster of banks to work on a potential initial public offering in Riyadh, according to people familiar with the matter, pressing ahead with listing plans despite lingering uncertainty over the war in Iran.

item.title: Canada Aims to Approve Projects in a Year After Criticism It’s Too Slow
item.description: The Canadian government is launching consultations on proposals to fast-track federal assessments of major projects, with the aim of completing reviews and making decisions within one year.

item.title: Odyssey Therapeutics Climbs 11% After $304 Million US IPO
item.description: Odyssey Therapeutics Inc. shares jumped 11% in its trading debut, after the biopharmaceutical company raised $304 million in an upsized US initial public offering and private placement.

item.title: OnlyFans Sells 16% Stake Weeks After Death of Owner Radvinsky
item.description: OnlyFans, the platform best known for adult content, agreed to sell a minority stake to Architect Capital in a deal that values the British company at about $3.15 billion.

item.title: Bitcoin Campaign to Force SNB Holdings Has Failed, Reuters Says
item.description: Swiss crypto campaigners have abandoned a bid to require the central bank to hold Bitcoin after failing to collect enough signatures to trigger a plebiscite, Reuters reported.

item.title: Orange Juice Jumps Most in a Week on Shrinking Brazil Crop
item.description: Orange juice futures surged in New York on an outlook for a weaker harvest of the fruit in Brazil, the world’s top exporter of the juice.

item.title: Siemens Scores Win on EU Push to Streamline Industrial AI Rules
item.description: Siemens AG and other European tech companies have made headway on reshaping regulation for artificial intelligence in the European Union they say is putting them behind rivals in the US and China.

item.title: Hormuz Transits Halted Since Tuesday as US, Iran Clash
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed to commercial shipping after the US and Iran clashed overnight near the waterway, attacking each other’s assets in the area.
